prerequisites: A reachable Microsoft SQL Server instance, with its host, port (default 1433), and target database name. host, port, and database are required config fields.
Network access from the machine running the connector to the SQL Server host and port. If the database is not directly reachable, configure tunnel_method to connect through an SSH bastion.
A login with at least SELECT (read) access on the schemas and tables you intend to replicate. Use the schemas config field to scope which schemas are read.
A replication_method choice (Standard cursor-based reads or CDC). replication_method is required. CDC requires SQL Server Change Data Capture to be enabled on the database and tables.
For SQL Server Authentication: the SQL Server instance must run in Mixed Mode (SQL Server and Windows Authentication) so that a SQL login and password can be used.

For Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) authentication: a registered Microsoft Entra application (service principal) with a client ID, a client secret, and the directory tenant ID; the connection must be encrypted.

authMethods.name: SQL Server Authentication (username and password)
authMethods.summary: Connect with a SQL Server login created and stored inside SQL Server, supplying the username and password config/secret fields. Per Microsoft, SQL Server logins are not based on Windows accounts and require the instance to be in Mixed Mode authentication. Maps to the password secret field (with the username config field).

authMethods.name: Microsoft Entra ID service principal
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as a registered Entra (Azure AD) application using its client_id, client_secret, and tenant_id. When these fields are set the connector uses Active Directory service principal authentication and requires an encrypted connection. Maps to the client_secret secret field (with the client_id and tenant_id config fields).

authMethods.name: Encrypted connection certificate (SSL)
authMethods.summary: Configure ssl_mode to require an encrypted connection. The ssl_mode.certificate secret holds the server certificate used to verify the SQL Server when using a verify-certificate SSL mode. This is a transport security option layered on top of the chosen login method, not a standalone identity.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel credentials
authMethods.summary: Optionally reach the database through an SSH bastion via tunnel_method. Authenticate to the bastion with either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or a tunnel user password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). This secures network access and is combined with one of the database login methods above.

setupSteps.title: Provide host, port, and database
setupSteps.body: Enter the SQL Server host and port (default 1433) and the target database name. These three fields are required. Confirm the instance is reachable from where the connector runs, or set up an SSH tunnel under tunnel_method if it is not directly accessible.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication mode
setupSteps.body: Use SQL Server Authentication by supplying a username and password for a SQL login (the instance must be in Mixed Mode per Microsoft Learn), or use Microsoft Entra ID by supplying client_id, client_secret, and tenant_id for a registered service principal. Entra ID authentication requires an encrypted connection.

setupSteps.title: Grant read access
setupSteps.body: Ensure the login has SELECT permission on the schemas and tables to be replicated. Optionally restrict the read surface with the schemas config field. Creating a dedicated read-only user is recommended.

setupSteps.title: Select a replication method
setupSteps.body: Set the required replication_method. Choose Standard for cursor-based full and incremental syncs, or CDC to use SQL Server Change Data Capture. CDC requires CDC to be enabled on the database and the relevant tables before syncing.

setupSteps.title: Configure encryption and tunneling (optional)
setupSteps.body: Set ssl_mode to require an encrypted connection, providing ssl_mode.certificate when verifying the server certificate. If connecting through a bastion, configure tunnel_method with the SSH host and either an SSH key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or a tunnel user password. Add any extra connection settings via jdbc_url_params.

sources.title: Choose an Authentication Mode - SQL Server | Microsoft Learn
